title: corybas laceratus
intro: Corybas laceratus, also known as the twayblade, is a type of orchid native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Corybas laceratus include alleviating respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as reducing fever and sore throats. The herbal preparation of Corybas laceratus can be made into a tea or infusion by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water, or it can be used in a tincture form.
